text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each integration is verified by an automated build and a set of automated tests. The process runs in a CI pipeline: every time a developer pushes new code, the system automatically compiles it, runs unit and integration tests, and checks for errors. This helps catch issues early, often within minutes of the change. By identifying problems quickly, teams can fix bugs before they become serious. CI leads to more stable code, faster feedback, and smoother collaboration among developers.
